questions.question: A small compass is placed near the centre of a bar magnet as shown in Figure 15.19. In which direction will the compass needle point?
options: Parallel to the magnet, pointing towards the South pole
Parallel to the magnet, pointing towards the North pole
Perpendicular to the magnet, pointing towards the top of the page
Perpendicular to the magnet, pointing towards the bottom of the page
questions.explanation:
Based on the review question in the notes, the field lines at the side of a bar magnet travel from North to South, parallel to the magnet's length.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which diagram correctly represents the magnetic field pattern of a bar magnet?
options: Curves directed away from the North pole and towards the South pole
Curves directed away from the South pole and towards the North pole
Straight lines pointing outwards from both poles
Concentric circles centred on the midpoint of the magnet
questions.explanation:
A standard bar magnet field consists of curves that exit the North pole and enter the South pole. Straight lines or concentric circles do not match this dipole pattern.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A student uses a plotting compass to map the magnetic field of a bar magnet. Which statement correctly describes the procedure?
options: The student marks points at the North end of the compass needle to trace the field direction
The student marks points at the South end of the compass needle to trace the field direction
The student must keep the compass at a fixed distance from the magnet at all times
The student must use a new compass for every new point marked
questions.explanation:
To map field lines, one follows the North-seeking end of the compass needle, marking its position and moving the compass South-end to that mark to continue the line.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A graph shows the magnetic field strength $B$ against the distance $d$ from the North pole of a magnet. Which of the following is true?
options: The field strength $B$ decreases as distance $d$ increases
The field strength $B$ increases as distance $d$ increases
The field strength $B$ remains constant as distance $d$ increases
The field strength $B$ is zero at the North pole
questions.explanation:
Magnetic force is strongest at the poles. As one moves further away (increasing $d$), the field lines spread out and the strength ($B$) decreases.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which row in the table correctly identifies properties of magnetic field lines?nnDirection | Indication of Strengthn--- | ---nA. North to South | Spacing of linesnB. South to North | Spacing of linesnC. North to South | Colour of linesnD. South to North | Length of lines
options: C
D
questions.explanation:
Field lines travel from North to South, and their relative spacing (density) indicates the strength of the magnetic field in that region.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
